title: Warhammer Quest - Take a Look - Part 13 - The White Dwarf Issues (e)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6VUBa5NSLU8

Daniel (@dungeondive) wraps up the White Dwarf magazine coverage for Warhammer Quest, revisiting issue 197's Lost Kingdoms ocean travel rules before moving through the final issues.

Issue 200 — the biggest White Dwarf ever — disappoints on the Quest front with only Fantasy Battle conversion rules, though Daniel highlights stunning Golden Demon paint jobs and more Space Hulk missions. Issue 201 revisits the Nurgle expansion "The Green and Pestilent Land" with its plague room monster tables and Chaos Sorcery spells. Issue 202 introduces "Out of the Darkness" about taking adventures above ground into castles and forests, plus features magnificent John Blanche artwork and Mike McVey dioramas.

Issue 204 includes a cardboard sewer tile and an event card for the Fascination of Slaanesh, plus "Room for Improvement" — rules for making individual dungeon rooms more thematic with unique encounters. The series culminates with issue 208, containing the final warrior release (the Bretonnian Questing Knight) and a poignant retrospective by creator Andy Jones, reflecting on two years of a game that took less time to create than fans had spent playing it. Daniel muses about a hypothetical dungeon crawl magazine that could keep games alive with mini-expansions — a sentiment that captures the community spirit Warhammer Quest inspired.

----

Should more designers write retrospectives on their games after a couple of years in the wild?
